growth_records.snippet: Fructose Wild-type OD660 7,8; C source Uptake 9,4 mmolC/gDW h; CO2 excretion 7,5; C in biomass 1,51; C balance 95,7%. Chemostat D=0.04, N-limited, 40 g/l fructose.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.04 /h (stated in Table 2 footnote). Doubling time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. European decimal commas in source (9,4 = 9.4). Uptake reported per carbon atom (mmolC/gDW/h); fructose has 6 C so qFru = 9.4/6 = 1.567 mmol fructose/gDW/h. CO2 is 1 C so mmolC=mmol directly = 7.5. No alginate produced (WT). No free acetate detected in medium. Temp 25C, pH 6.8 (NaOH), aeration 0.5 vvm, DO 20%. Full Def4 salt composition in Additional file 4, not in main-text XML.

growth_records.cite: Borgos et al. Mapping global effects of the anti-sigma factor MucA in Pseudomonas fluorescens SBW25 through genome-scale metabolic modeling. BMC Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-7-19. [PMC3641028]
growth_records.snippet: Fructose mucA OD660 7,0; C source Uptake 18,3 mmolC/gDW h; Alginate (deacetylated) 12,3; Acetate (acetyl from alginate) 0,77; CO2 4,6; C in biomass 1,51; C balance 104,4%.
growth_records.notes: mu = D = 0.04 /h; td = 17.33 h. Uptake per carbon: qFru = 18.3/6 = 3.05 mmol fructose/gDW/h. Alginate secretion reported as mmolC/gDW/h; uronic-acid monomer has 6 C so ~12.3/6 = 2.05 mmol monomer/gDW/h. Acetyl (0.77 mmolC) is O-acetylation on alginate (degree 0.19 on fructose), NOT free excreted acetate ('No free acetate was detected in the culture growth media'). mucA raises fructose uptake to feed alginate carbon drain while respiration (CO2) drops vs WT.

growth_records.snippet: The specific rates for fructose uptake and biomass production in nitrogen-limited chemostats are shown in Table 1... wild-type1) OD660 8.0+/-0.1, Specific fructose uptake 11.0+/-0.6 mmolC/gDW h, Specific CO2 excretion 9.6+/-0.61 mmol/gDW h,
growth_records.notes: Chemostat CLE; steady-state mu = dilution rate D = 0.04/h. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.04 = 17.33 h. Uptake and CO2 excretion reported in Table 1 in mmolC (carbon-mole) per gDW/h; fructose has 6 C, so molar fructose uptake = 11.0/6 = 1.83 mmol fructose/gDW/h (derived). Specific biomass production 1.4+/-0.13 mmolC/gDW/h (calculated from growth rate + biomass equation). CO2 excretion footnoted as 'difference between fructose uptake and biomass production'; no organic acids detected in supernatant. Model-estimated (simulation) rates for this cultivation: fructose 11.76+/-0.54, CO2 10.41+/-0.55 mmolC/gDW/h. Temperature 25C, pH 7.8 (NaOH control), air 1.5 VVM, O2>20%. Feed 25 g/L fructose (WT), ~2.5 g/L residual. This is the wild-type experimental-design (pre-CLE) cultivation, distinct from the main-experiment WT cultivation.

growth_records.notes: Main-experiment wild-type chemostat CLE (OD660 7.0). Steady-state mu = D = 0.04/h; doubling time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Uptake/CO2 in Table 1 in mmolC/gDW/h; molar fructose uptake = 11.8/6 = 1.97 mmol fructose/gDW/h (derived). Biomass 1.4+/-0.13 mmolC/gDW/h. Model-estimated rates matched (Table 1). MFA net fluxes (13CFLUX2, model iSB1139-derived, 66 metabolites/116 reactions) from text/branch-point analysis; full flux map in Additional file 2 (not in body). Absolute flux values are mmol/gDW/h. NOTE: a previously published metabolome study (ref 9) reported wild-type fructose uptake 9.4 mmolC/gDW/h and a mucA- single mutant 18.3 mmolC/gDW/h (cited from another paper, not measured here).

growth_records.notes: Main-experiment mutant chemostat CLE (OD660 8.9). Steady-state mu = D = 0.04/h; doubling time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Fructose uptake 7.34+/-0.4 mmolC/gDW/h (~40% lower than wild type 11.8); molar = 7.34/6 = 1.22 mmol fructose/gDW/h (derived). Same biomass rate (1.4 mmolC/gDW/h) as wild type but lower substrate uptake -> Yxs 19% vs 12% (60% higher). Model-estimated rates: fructose 7.39+/-0.32, CO2 6.08+/-0.34, biomass 1.30+/-0.13 mmolC/gDW/h. Feed 20 g/L fructose. MFA net fluxes (13CFLUX2) show EDP down, PPP up, GLX shunt activated, PYR shunt down vs wild type; absolute flux units mmol/gDW/h. No organic-acid by-products detected.
